title: Xtra Light British Seaweed Salad
description: A briny, lazy‑Sunday British dish featuring a rice wine glaze and Buddhist‑temple‑friendly ingredients.
instructions: Soak 200g dried kombu and 50g dried wakame in 1 liter of cold water for 10 minutes until rehydrated.
While the seaweed rehydrates, peel and julienne 1 cucumber and grate 1 carrot into thin ribbons.
In a small bowl whisk together 1 tbsp rice wine vinegar, 1 tsp honey, 1 tsp soy sauce, and a pinch of chili flakes to create the glaze.
Heat 1 tbsp sesame oil in a non‑stick pan over medium heat, then add 1 tsp freshly grated ginger and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Drain the rehydrated seaweed, rinse briefly with cold water, and cut into bite‑sized pieces.
Toss the seaweed with cucumber, carrot, and the warm ginger‑sesame oil mixture in a large bowl.
Drizzle the prepared glaze over the salad, gently toss to coat evenly, then sprinkle 1 tsp toasted sesame seeds on top.
Chill the salad in the refrigerator for 10 minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld.
